name: ETH Trend Reset Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for a trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. The edge should come from a full reset into support that restores risk/reward while preserving directional structure.
invalidation_condition: Abort if the reset condition is still too rare, if deeper pullbacks mostly continue into full reversals, or if the continuation leg is too weak after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: An existing directional structure absorbs a deeper-than-normal pullback, then resumes once value is reclaimed and momentum pressure stabilizes.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Weak trend-up conditions often punish shallow buy-the-dip logic but still reward deeper resets that force late longs out before continuation resumes. This should produce denser participation than exact single-bar continuation triggers.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the better home than TAO for this concept because the reset/continuation structure is usually cleaner and less gap-like, which helps separate the pullback condition from the resumption trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the broader tape is still too rotational, deeper pullbacks may only bounce to value and fail to expand into continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but the runner gives back too much, keep the same entry structure and replace the runner with a time-conditioned scale-out.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; depth, recapture, and trend participation are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by allowing moderate deep-pullback thresholds rather than crash-only resets

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14 by selling 20%
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14 by covering 20%
